# GDPR & Privacy Compliance Helper for Small Websites

*Generated by Forge on 2026-04-17T06:47:04.390Z — score 8.2/10*

## Overview
A lightweight web SaaS for indie developers, freelancers, agencies, and small website owners to run a fast privacy compliance audit, see what is missing, and track fixes over time. The problem is not that small sites need enterprise-grade legal software; it is that they need a practical way to check basics like consent banners, policy links, tracking scripts, forms, data flows, retention notes, and audit evidence without paying for a full compliance suite. The solution is a self-serve “privacy audit + fix tracker” app that combines automated website scanning with a guided checklist and evidence capture, positioned clearly for small websites rather than as a generic GDPR platform.

## Target user
Primary persona: a solo founder, freelance web developer, or small agency owner managing 1–20 websites.

Who they are:
- They run marketing sites, SaaS landing pages, ecommerce stores, or brochure websites.
- They use common tools like Google Analytics, Meta Pixel, Stripe, Mailchimp, HubSpot, Typeform, Calendly, Intercom, or contact forms.
- They are not lawyers and do not want to become compliance experts.

What they currently do:
- Manually check whether a site has a privacy policy, cookie banner, consent settings, and form disclosures.
- Use spreadsheets, Notion docs, or ad hoc task lists to track compliance work.
- Sometimes rely on cookie banner vendors or policy generators, but those tools do not give them a practical audit workflow across all privacy basics.
- For client work, they take screenshots and save loose notes in Google Drive or Slack.

Why current options fail them:
- Enterprise tools are too expensive and too broad.
- Cookie-banner-first tools solve only one slice of the problem.
- Legal doc generators do not help them identify missing implementation details on the website.
- Existing compliance suites often feel heavy, legalistic, or overbuilt for a small operator who just wants to know: what is missing, how serious is it, and what should I fix first?

## Core features
- User authentication with Google sign-in and email-based account support later if needed.
- Website/project management:
  - Create a project with site name, domain, region focus (EU/global), and notes.
  - View all projects in a simple dashboard.
- Automated homepage/site scan:
  - Fetch and analyze the homepage HTML.
  - Detect presence of privacy policy link, cookie policy link, terms link, contact form, newsletter/email signup form, analytics scripts, common marketing pixels, chat widgets, embedded third-party scripts, and payment provider references.
  - Store scan timestamp and findings.
- Rule-based privacy checks:
  - Generate a checklist status from scan results:
    - Privacy policy present/missing
    - Cookie/consent banner likely present/missing
    - Analytics detected
    - Marketing trackers detected
    - Forms collecting personal data detected
    - Payment/data processor indicators detected
    - Third-party embeds/scripts detected
  - Mark each item as Pass / Warning / Fail / Needs manual review.
- Manual audit checklist:
  - Editable checklist for non-detectable items:
    - Data retention documented
    - Data processor list documented
    - Legal basis documented
    - DSAR/contact process documented
    - Consent logging process defined
    - Cookie categories documented
  - User can mark status, add notes, and assign due dates.
- Remediation tracker:
  - Convert failed/warning items into tasks.
  - Task fields: title, severity, status, owner, due date, notes.
  - Kanban is not needed; simple list view only.
- Evidence capture:
  - Upload screenshots/files for each project or checklist item.
  - Add URL evidence and text notes.
  - Store uploaded files in S3-compatible storage.
- Audit history:
  - Keep scan history per project.
  - Show “last scan vs current scan” summary.
- Basic compliance score:
  - Simple weighted score out of 100 based on checklist completion and scan findings.
  - Show top 3 missing items to fix first.
- PDF export:
  - Export a simple audit summary PDF with project info, findings, checklist status, tasks, and evidence references.
- Billing/subscriptions:
  - Free tier with one project and limited scans.
  - Paid plan for more projects, recurring scans, exports, and evidence storage.
- Email notifications:
  - Send welcome email.
  - Send weekly digest for overdue tasks and scan reminders.
- Marketing site:
  - Landing page, pricing page, feature page, blog, and free website privacy check lead capture page.

## Tech stack recommendation
Use the standard web stack. This is a browser-based B2B SaaS with dashboards, scans, storage, recurring subscriptions, and SEO-driven acquisition. It does not require native mobile capabilities. A web app ships faster, supports search acquisition, and matches how users manage websites.

Recommended stack:
- Next.js 15.x (App Router) + TypeScript 5.x
- PostgreSQL 16 + Prisma ORM 6.x
- Tailwind CSS 3.4+ + shadcn/ui
- NextAuth v5 (`next-auth@beta` / Auth.js v5) with Google OAuth
- Stripe for subscriptions and customer portal
- Resend for transactional email
- Coolify deployment via Docker
- S3-compatible object storage for evidence uploads:
  - Prefer Cloudflare R2 or Backblaze B2
- Background job processing:
  - Use database-backed scheduled jobs initially via Vercel/cron-equivalent is not ideal on Coolify, so implement app-level scheduled route plus server cron in Coolify hitting internal endpoints
- PDF generation:
  - `@react-pdf/renderer` or HTML-to-PDF via Playwright
- HTML parsing and scanning:
  - `cheerio`
- HTTP fetching:
  - native `fetch` with timeout wrapper, optionally `axios` if preferred
- File upload:
  - `uploadthing` is optional, but for simplicity use presigned S3 upload flow
- Validation/forms:
  - `zod`, `react-hook-form`
- Tables:
  - `@tanstack/react-table`
- Date handling:
  - `date-fns`
- Charts:
  - `recharts` for simple score/history chart
- Logging/error tracking:
  - `pino` or `console` initially; optional Sentry later, not MVP
- Optional crawler helpers:
  - `robots-parser` to respect robots.txt for deeper scans if added later

Why this stack:
- SEO matters, so Next.js is the right delivery model.
- PostgreSQL + Prisma keeps relational project/checklist/task/evidence data easy to manage.
- Stripe and NextAuth are standard and fast to integrate.
- Coolify is cost-effective for a solo founder.
- The scanner can be implemented server-side without needing browser extension or native app complexity.

## Monetization
Primary strategy: freemium with one paid subscription tier.

Why:
- A free website privacy check is the best acquisition wedge.
- Users need to see value before paying because “compliance helper” is a trust-sensitive category.
- One paid tier keeps product and billing simple for a solo developer.

Pricing:
- Free
  - 1 project
  - 1 manual scan per month
  - Basic checklist
  - No PDF export
  - Up to 3 evidence files
- Pro — $19/month or $190/year
  - Up to 10 projects
  - Unlimited manual scans
  - Weekly automated scan reminders
  - Full checklist and remediation tracker
  - PDF export
  - 2 GB evidence storage
  - Audit history
- Agency — $59/month or $590/year
  - Up to 50 projects
  - Team/client-friendly project management without full multi-user collaboration in MVP
  - Branded PDF export
  - 10 GB evidence storage
  - Priority support

If keeping it even simpler at launch:
- Launch with Free + Pro only
- Hide Agency plan until usage validates demand

## Revenue model
Detailed pricing strategy:
- Free tier drives signups and SEO conversion.
- Pro at $19/month is priced to be an easy purchase for solo founders and freelancers.
- Agency at $59/month captures higher-value users managing multiple client sites.
- Annual plans at ~2 months discount improve cash flow.

Assumptions:
- Free-to-paid conversion: 3% by month 3, 4–5% by month 12
- Paid mix:
  - 85% Pro
  - 15% Agency
- Average revenue per paid user (blended):
  - Roughly $25/month if Agency is visible
  - Roughly $19/month if only Pro exists
- Traffic acquisition starts with free audit pages and founder/community distribution

Projected users and revenue:

Month 1
- 150 total signups
- 140 free
- 10 paid
  - 8 Pro = $152 MRR
  - 2 Agency = $118 MRR
- Total MRR: $270

Month 3
- 700 total signups
- 670 free
- 30 paid
  - 25 Pro = $475 MRR
  - 5 Agency = $295 MRR
- Total MRR: $770

Month 6
- 2,000 total signups
- 1,920 free
- 80 paid
  - 68 Pro = $1,292 MRR
  - 12 Agency = $708 MRR
- Total MRR: $2,000

Month 12
- Conservative case:
  - 6,000 total signups
  - 5,760 free
  - 240 paid
  - 204 Pro = $3,876 MRR
  - 36 Agency = $2,124 MRR
  - Total MRR: $6,000
- Optimistic case:
  - 14,000 total signups
  - 13,500 free
  - 500 paid
  - 425 Pro = $8,075 MRR
  - 75 Agency = $4,425 MRR
  - Total MRR: $12,500

Upper optimistic path from provided market estimate:
- If SEO and agency referrals work well, 400–700 mixed paid accounts can support roughly $12,000–$30,000 MRR depending on plan mix and annual conversions.

Conversion funnel:
- Visitor to free signup: 3–8% on tool-oriented pages
- Free signup to first project created: 60%
- Project created to first scan run: 75%
- First scan to paid conversion: 5–12% among activated users
- Overall free-to-paid: 3–5%

## Marketing plan
Launch strategy for week 1:
- Ship a public free website privacy check landing page with email capture and limited scan.
- Post launch threads in founder and freelancer communities.
- Publish 3 highly practical SEO pages and 2 comparison posts.
- Personally outreach to freelancers/agencies offering free beta access for client-site audits.

Channel-by-channel plan:

SEO/content
Pages to create in first 30 days:
- Homepage
- Pricing
- Free website privacy check
- GDPR checklist app page
- For agencies page
- Blog:
  - GDPR checklist for startup websites
  - How to check if your website needs a consent banner
  - Website privacy audit checklist for freelancers
  - What to document besides your privacy policy
  - Cookie banner vs full privacy compliance

Content strategy:
- Focus on high-intent operational searches, not generic legal education.
- Every article should end with a CTA to run a free audit or start a checklist.
- Include screenshots of scan results and checklist output.

Community seeding
Specific communities:
- Indie Hackers
- r/SaaS
- r/Entrepreneur

- r/smallbusiness
- r/ecommerce
- Hacker News “Show HN”

- Webflow, Framer, Shopify, and WordPress communities
- Agency owner Slack/Discord groups

Approach:
- Do not pitch as “be compliant instantly.”
- Pitch as “fast privacy audit + fix tracker for small websites.”
- Share example audits and practical checklists.

Social media
- X/Twitter: build in public with scan examples, before/after audits, and checklists
- LinkedIn: target freelancers, agencies, and startup operators with educational posts
- Short-form demo videos:
  - “Audit a website’s privacy basics in 60 seconds”
  - “3 things most startup sites miss”

Paid acquisition
- Initial budget: $500–$1,500 over first 3 months
- Use only for retargeting and high-intent search terms after landing pages are live
- Target keywords:
  - website privacy check
  - gdpr checklist app
  - privacy compliance tracker
  - consent banner check
- Expected CAC:
  - Organic/community: low cash CAC, higher time cost
  - Paid search: $25–$120 CAC
- Paid should be secondary until conversion data is clear

3-month marketing budget and ROI
- Content writing/design/tools: $300–$1,000
- Paid search experiments: $500–$2,000
- Sponsorships/listings/tools: $200–$1,000
- Total: $1,000–$4,000 lean plan, up to $6,000 if testing paid more aggressively

Expected ROI:
- If 30 paid users by month 3 at ~$25 blended MRR, MRR is ~$750.
- Payback is not immediate on paid ads alone, so prioritize SEO and community first.
- Best ROI likely comes from free audit pages converting agency/freelancer users who bring multiple projects.

## Competitor landscape
### Termly
Pricing model:
- Subscription SaaS, typically tiered by features/site needs

Key features:
- Policy generators
- Cookie consent management
- Compliance tools across multiple regulations

Weaknesses:
- Broader product scope makes it feel heavier and more expensive than many small site owners need
- Strong emphasis on policy generation and consent management, less focused on a simple audit-first workflow
- Can push users toward setup complexity before they understand what is missing

How we differentiate:
- Start with scan results and remediation order
- Focus on “what to fix first” for small websites
- Simpler dashboard and evidence tracking for practical audits

### Cookiebot by Usercentrics
Pricing model:
- Subscription, often based on pages/domains/consent management needs

Key features:
- Cookie scanning
- Consent banner management
- Consent logging and compliance tooling

Weaknesses:
- Positioned primarily around cookie consent and scanning
- Not a lightweight all-in-one privacy operations tracker for small sites
- Can feel enterprise-leaning or overbuilt for indie founders and small agencies

How we differentiate:
- Broader than cookies, but still lightweight
- Covers policy links, forms, trackers, data processors, retention notes, and evidence
- Better fit for freelancers auditing client sites end-to-end

### Iubenda
Pricing model:
- Subscription with multiple legal/compliance products and add-ons

Key features:
- Legal documents
- Consent management
- Compliance support across regulations

Weaknesses:
- Wide feature set creates setup complexity
- Can feel like a legal product suite rather than an operational audit tool
- Opportunity for clearer evidence capture and remediation checklists tailored to non-legal users

How we differentiate:
- Built for non-lawyers
- Checklist and task workflow first
- Clear “scan, review, fix, document” flow

### Our positioning
We should not position as a generic “GDPR compliance platform.” That puts the app directly against larger suites. The sharper positioning is:
- Fast privacy audit + fix tracker for small websites
- Built for indie founders, freelancers, and agencies
- Practical checks, remediation tasks, and evidence capture
- Simpler and cheaper than enterprise compliance suites
- Broader than cookie-banner vendors without becoming a legal platform

## Timeline
### Week 1-2: core MVP
Week 1
- Set up Next.js app, auth, database, UI shell
- Create landing page, pricing page, dashboard shell
- Implement user accounts and project CRUD
- Define Prisma schema for projects, scans, checklist items, tasks, evidence, subscriptions
- Build manual checklist UI
- Build scan job trigger and basic homepage fetch

Week 2
- Implement HTML parser and detection rules
- Save scan findings and generate pass/warn/fail results
- Build findings UI and compliance score
- Create remediation task list from findings
- Add evidence upload and notes
- Add free tier limits
- Add Stripe checkout and billing portal

### Week 3: polish + testing
- Add audit history and scan comparison
- Add PDF export
- Add weekly reminder emails
- Improve empty states, onboarding, and dashboard UX
- Add validation, rate limiting, and error handling for scanner
- Test on real websites across common stacks: Shopify, WordPress, Next.js, Webflow

### Week 4: beta + marketing prep
- Invite 10–20 beta users from founder/freelancer communities
- Fix false positives/false negatives in scanner rules
- Add public free audit landing page
- Write first 5 SEO articles
- Set up analytics, event tracking, and conversion tracking
- Prepare Product Hunt assets and launch copy

### Week 5: launch + distribution
- Launch on Product Hunt, Indie Hackers, X, LinkedIn, and Show HN
- Publish comparison content vs cookie-banner tools
- Start direct outreach to agencies and freelancers
- Collect user feedback and prioritize:
  - More detection rules
  - Better exports
  - Agency workflows

## Score breakdown
- recurringRevenue: 8/10 — Compliance is ongoing, so recurring audits, storage, and tracking support subscriptions well.
- buildComplexity: 7/10 — The app is very buildable, but scanner reliability, evidence storage, and billing add moderate complexity.
- marketValidation: 8/10 — Search intent and existing paid tools show clear demand for privacy-related website operations.
- competition: 4/10 — The market is crowded with cookie and compliance vendors, so positioning must stay narrow and practical.
- automationPotential: 7/10 — Scanning, reminders, task generation, and periodic audits can be automated meaningfully.
- platformFit: 10/10 — This is a strong fit for a web SaaS because the workflow is dashboard-based and SEO/discovery matter.

## Build instructions for Codex
1. Initialize project:
   - Run `npx create-next-app@latest gdpr-privacy-compliance-helper-small-websites --typescript --tailwind --eslint --app --src-dir --import-alias "@/*"`
   - Configure `next.config.ts` with `output: "standalone"`.

2. Install dependencies:
   - Run `npm install prisma @prisma/client next-auth @auth/prisma-adapter stripe resend zod react-hook-form @hookform/resolvers cheerio date-fns @tanstack/react-table recharts @react-pdf/renderer`
   - Run `npm install @aws-sdk/client-s3 @aws-sdk/s3-request-presigner`
   - Run `npm install lucide-react sonner bcryptjs`
   - Run `npm install -D tsx`

3. Install and configure shadcn/ui:
   - Run `npx shadcn@latest init`
   - Add components: `button`, `card`, `dialog`, `dropdown-menu`, `form`, `input`, `label`, `select`, `table`, `textarea`, `badge`, `tabs`, `sheet`, `calendar`, `popover`, `progress`, `sonner`, `checkbox`.

4. Set up Prisma:
   - Run `npx prisma init`
   - Create models in `prisma/schema.prisma`:
     - `User`
     - `Account`
     - `Session`
     - `VerificationToken`
     - `Project`
     - `ScanRun`
     - `ScanFinding`
     - `ChecklistItem`
     - `Task`
     - `Evidence`
     - `Subscription`
   - Include enums:
     - `ProjectRegion`
     - `FindingStatus`
     - `ChecklistStatus`
     - `TaskStatus`
     - `Severity`
     - `PlanType`
   - Run `npx prisma generate` and `npx prisma migrate dev`.

5. Define data shapes in Prisma:
   - `Project`: `id`, `userId`, `name`, `domain`, `region`, `notes`, `createdAt`, `updatedAt`
   - `ScanRun`: `id`, `projectId`, `status`, `score`, `startedAt`, `completedAt`, `rawHtmlSnippet?`, `summaryJson`
   - `ScanFinding`: `id`, `scanRunId`, `key`, `label`, `status`, `severity`, `details`, `detectedValue?`
   - `ChecklistItem`: `id`, `projectId`, `key`, `label`, `status`, `notes`, `dueDate?`, `isManual`, `lastReviewedAt?`
   - `Task`: `id`, `projectId`, `findingKey?`, `title`, `severity`, `status`, `dueDate?`, `notes`
   - `Evidence`: `id`, `projectId`, `checklistItemId?`, `taskId?`, `type`, `fileName?`, `fileUrl?`, `sourceUrl?`, `note?`
   - `Subscription`: `id`, `userId`, `stripeCustomerId`, `stripeSubscriptionId`, `plan`, `status`, `currentPeriodEnd`

6. Configure database and Prisma client:
   - Create `src/lib/db.ts` exporting singleton Prisma client.
   - Add environment variables in `.env` for `DATABASE_URL`.

7. Configure NextAuth v5:
   - Create `src/auth.ts` with Auth.js config using Prisma adapter and Google provider.
   - Create `src/app/api/auth/[...nextauth]/route.ts`.
   - Add `middleware.ts` or route protection helpers for `/app/*`.
   - Add env vars: `AUTH_SECRET`, `AUTH_GOOGLE_ID`, `AUTH_GOOGLE_SECRET`.

8. Build app shell and routes:
   - Marketing pages:
     - `src/app/page.tsx`
     - `src/app/pricing/page.tsx`
     - `src/app/website-privacy-check/page.tsx`
     - `src/app/gdpr-checklist/page.tsx`
     - `src/app/for-agencies/page.tsx`
   - Authenticated app:
     - `src/app/app/layout.tsx`
     - `src/app/app/dashboard/page.tsx`
     - `src/app/app/projects/page.tsx`
     - `src/app/app/projects/[projectId]/page.tsx`
     - `src/app/app/settings/billing/page.tsx`

9. Create project CRUD:
   - Build `ProjectForm` component in `src/components/projects/project-form.tsx`.
   - Server actions or route handlers for create/update/delete project.
   - Fields: name, domain, region, notes.
   - Add project list table with last scan date, score, open tasks.

10. Implement scanner service:
   - Create `src/lib/scanner/fetch-site.ts` with timeout and basic URL normalization.
   - Create `src/lib/scanner/detect-rules.ts` using Cheerio.
   - Detect:
     - privacy policy links by anchor text/href
     - cookie policy links
     - terms links
     - consent banner keywords in HTML
     - analytics scripts: GA4, GTM, Plausible, Fathom
     - marketing pixels: Meta Pixel, LinkedIn Insight, TikTok Pixel
     - forms: `form`, email inputs, newsletter copy
     - payment providers: Stripe checkout references, PayPal
     - embeds/widgets: YouTube, Vimeo, Calendly, Typeform, HubSpot, Intercom
   - Return normalized findings array and score.

11. Add scan execution flow:
   - Create route `src/app/api/projects/[projectId]/scan/route.ts` to trigger scan.
   - Save `ScanRun` and `ScanFinding` rows.
   - Create helper to upsert baseline `ChecklistItem` records from findings.
   - Enforce free-tier usage limits before running scan.

12. Build project detail UI:
   - Sections on `src/app/app/projects/[projectId]/page.tsx`:
     - project header
     - current score card
     - latest findings list
     - manual checklist
     - remediation tasks
     - evidence list
     - scan history
   - Create components:
     - `ComplianceScoreCard`
     - `FindingsTable`
     - `ManualChecklistPanel`
     - `TaskList`
     - `EvidenceUploader`
     - `ScanHistoryList`

13. Implement manual checklist:
   - Seed default checklist keys on project creation:
     - `data_retention_documented`
     - `processors_documented`
     - `legal_basis_documented`
     - `dsar_process_documented`
     - `consent_logging_defined`
     - `cookie_categories_documented`
   - Allow user to set status, notes, due date.
   - Persist via server actions.

14. Implement remediation tracker:
   - Add button “Create tasks from findings”.
   - Generate tasks for all `Fail` and selected `Warning` findings if task does not already exist.
   - Build simple list UI with filters by status and severity.
   - No multi-user assignment in MVP; `owner` can be free-text or omitted.

15. Implement evidence uploads:
   - Create S3 helper in `src/lib/storage.ts`.
   - Create route for presigned upload URL: `src/app/api/uploads/presign/route.ts`.
   - Store uploaded file metadata in `Evidence`.
   - Support note-only evidence and source URL evidence.
   - Show thumbnails/links where possible.

16. Add Stripe billing:
   - Create Stripe products/prices for Free, Pro, Agency.
   - Build checkout route `src/app/api/stripe/checkout/route.ts`.
   - Build billing portal route `src/app/api/stripe/portal/route.ts`.
   - Add webhook route `src/app/api/webhooks/stripe/route.ts`.
   - Sync subscription status into `Subscription` table.
   - Gate features:
     - free project count
     - scan limits
     - PDF export
     - storage quota

17. Add email flows with Resend:
   - Create `src/lib/email.ts`.
   - Send welcome email after signup.
   - Create weekly digest endpoint/worker for overdue tasks and scan reminders.
   - Templates:
     - `WelcomeEmail`
     - `WeeklyDigestEmail`

18. Implement PDF export:
   - Create `src/lib/pdf/audit-report.tsx`.
   - Export project summary including:
     - project info
     - latest score
     - findings
     - checklist statuses
     - tasks
     - evidence references
   - Add export button on project page for paid users only.

19. Add analytics, limits, and polish:
   - Track events:
     - signup
     - project_created
     - scan_run
     - task_created
     - upgrade_started
     - upgrade_completed
   - Add rate limiting on scan endpoint by user and project.
   - Add robust loading/error states.
   - Add robots-friendly SEO metadata to marketing pages.
   - Add sample blog content pages under `src/app/blog/[slug]/page.tsx`.

20. Final verification and deployment:
   - Ensure `npm run lint` and `npm run build` pass.
   - Create `Dockerfile` for standalone Next.js app.
   - Create `docker-compose` or Coolify-compatible config if needed.
   - Set production env vars for database, auth, Stripe, Resend, S3.
   - Run Prisma migrations in deploy pipeline.
   - Deploy to Coolify.
   - Smoke test:
     - sign in
     - create project
     - run scan
     - edit checklist
     - upload evidence
     - start Stripe checkout
     - receive webhook
     - export PDF
